title: GET /jobs list + GET /stats endpoints
description: Dashboard needs: GET /jobs (list all jobs with optional ?state, ?limit, ?offset filters) and GET /stats (today_cost_cents, today_jobs_count, all_time_cost_cents). Implementation reads from the SQLite jobs + daily_usage tables. Returns JSON. Tests cover empty state, filtering, ordering by created_at desc.
acceptance_criteria: GET /jobs returns paged job list; GET /stats returns aggregate counters. Pytest covers both.
notes: DONE: GET /jobs (list, paged, state-filtered) + GET /stats (today/all-time/queue). list_jobs + aggregate_stats methods on SQLiteQueue. Parameterized SQL only. 9 new tests; FastAPI Query bounds limit at 200 via 422.

title: Accessibility-lead review pass (gate before v0.6.5 close)
description: Run accessibility-lead subagent against every screen: dashboard, job detail, settings, manual translate form, glossary editor, library coverage matrix. WCAG 2.2 AA target. Keyboard-only navigation must work end-to-end. Screen reader announcements correct for live regions (queue updates, job state changes). Color contrast ≥4.5:1. Focus indicators visible. Forms have proper labels + error messages tied via aria-describedby.
acceptance_criteria: accessibility-lead signs off. Findings remediated or documented as known limitations. Lighthouse accessibility score ≥95 on every screen.

title: SQLite jobs schema + migrations (server/queue/sqlite.py)
description: Hand-rolled idempotent migrations. schema_version table + ordered MIGRATIONS list. v0.1.5 introduces jobs + daily_usage tables. WAL mode for concurrency. SQLite path from env (DB_PATH, default <APP_DATA>/translarr.db).
acceptance_criteria: On server start, missing migrations apply in transaction. schema_version row reflects current. Tests cover apply-from-empty and apply-from-vN.
notes: DONE: server/db.py + server/queue/sqlite.py. Hand-rolled migrations, WAL mode, atomic claim. 11 tests cover the queue surface.

title: Async /translate + GET /jobs/{id}
description: v0.1.5 flips /translate from sync to async: returns {status:queued, job_id} immediately. Worker pool picks up queued jobs and runs them through translate_media. Client polls GET /jobs/{id} for state + result. DELETE /jobs/{id} cancels in-flight jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint contract matches design doc spec. Polling shows running → done. Restart mid-job resumes via checkpoint.
notes: DONE: /translate flipped to async (returns job_id). /jobs/{id} GET + DELETE. Worker pool starts via FastAPI lifespan. /translate/sync kept for back-compat. 6 endpoint tests.

title: Queue interface (server/queue/base.py)
description: Abstract Queue protocol: enqueue(req) -> job_id, claim() -> job|None, update(job_id, state, ...), checkpoint(job_id, last_completed_line), get(job_id). Designed so SQLite is one driver, Redis is a future driver. Module-level singleton accessed via server.queue.get_queue().
acceptance_criteria: Protocol defined. SQLite driver passes basic enqueue/claim/update flow. Tests cover the interface.
notes: DONE: server/queue/base.py with Job dataclass + JobState enum + Queue Protocol.

title: v0.1.5 — Persistent queue + jobs table
description: Replace in-memory dedup queue with SQLite-backed queue. Jobs survive server restart. State observable via /jobs/{id}. /translate becomes async (returns job_id; client polls). Includes daily_usage table for cost-tracker persistence. Queue interface designed for future Redis driver swap (v0.6+ if multi-process needed).
acceptance_criteria: Server restart mid-job resumes correctly. /jobs/{id} returns state. Cancellation via DELETE /jobs/{id}. All v0.1 cost guards survive restart via daily_usage table. Tests cover claim races, retry/backoff, resume-from-checkpoint.
notes: v0.1.5 SHIPPED 2026-05-14. 42/42 tests green. NOTE: mid-job checkpoint for true resume-from-line-N not yet implemented — retries currently restart from line 0. Filed as follow-up if needed.

title: Cost + runtime kill-switches
description: Implement MAX_COST_CENTS_PER_DAY (default 1000), JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 1800), MAX_COST_CENTS_PER_JOB (default 500) env vars. Daily counter in-memory v0.1, SQLite daily_usage table v0.1.5. Spec in design doc '/translate HTTP contract' section.
acceptance_criteria: Exceeding any cap returns documented error code; per-job timeout marks job failed.
notes: DONE: cost_tracker module with daily + per-job caps. Wired into pipeline at batch boundaries. Test coverage in test_cost_tracker.py.

title: Output collision policy: <basename>.<lang>.translarr.srt
description: Use .translarr. infix in output filename. Never overwrite .<lang>.srt without infix. force=true backs up to .<timestamp>.bak.srt. Spec in design doc 'Output collision policy'.
acceptance_criteria: Existing output not overwritten unless force=true; backup created on force.
notes: DONE: output uses .translarr.srt infix to avoid collision with human/Bazarr subs. force=true backs up existing to .<timestamp>.bak.srt. AlreadyTranslated exception → HTTP 409.

title: Tag parsing for radarr_translate / sonarr_translate
description: Webhook handlers read Radarr 'movie.tags[].label' and Sonarr 'series.tags[].label' arrays. If 'radarr_translate' (or sonarr_translate) tag is present, enqueue translation. Otherwise return {status: ignored, reason: no_translate_tag}. Spec in design doc 'Webhook payload mapping' section.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook with tag triggers job; webhook without tag returns ignored.
notes: DONE: tag-parsing in radarr.py + sonarr.py. Accepts both string-form and dict-form tag arrays. Test event responds 200 OK.

title: v0.1.25 — Emby plugin extension-point spike
description: Validate Emby plugin context-menu surface BEFORE committing v0.2 plugin work. Build a minimal C# DLL that registers a context-menu item (or proves it can't be done) on any media item type. Outcome determines v0.2 UX shape: real context menu vs Translate button on item page.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) working DLL that adds context menu item to Emby web UI, or (b) documented impossibility with the chosen-fallback UX spec'd for v0.2.
notes:
SPIKE OUTCOME 2026-05-14: Emby plugin SDK does NOT support declarative context-menu items or per-item action buttons. Confirmed via Luke (Emby Team) on the Emby Community forums. Plugins are server-side only — they cannot inject UI elements into Emby's web frontend.

RECOMMENDED v0.2 UX (research source: subagent report archived in session): Hybrid pattern combining:
1. IScheduledTask 'Translarr: Batch translate library' — appears in Emby Dashboard > Scheduled Tasks. User-triggered or scheduled.
2. IService REST controller POST /Translarr/Translate?itemId=X&targetLang=Y — for external automation and future plugin-internal callers.
3. Auto-generated settings page (IHasWebPages) — configure target lang, model, API URL, etc.
4. NO per-item button injection. Document that users invoke translation via Dashboard task or external API.

Real-world precedent: Emby.Jimaku plugin works the same way (no UI injection; surfaces via the built-in Edit Subtitles modal's provider list).

Same constraint applies to v0.3 Jellyfin plugin per the research.

title: Scheduled task: batch translate
description: Implement IScheduledTask 'Translarr: Batch translate' — user picks library + target language + filter, task iterates items and submits each via the REST controller. Runs in the background, reports progress to Emby's task list.
acceptance_criteria: Task shows up in Scheduled Tasks. Running it processes a small test library end-to-end. Progress updates visible.
notes:
Elevated to P1 2026-05-14: scheduled task is now the PRIMARY user-facing entry point for the Emby plugin (no context menu possible). Per-show auto-translate flag UI (TR-7p7.2.7) feeds the same task via the series_flags table.
DONE: BatchTranslateTask : IScheduledTask. Daily trigger at 04:00 default. Queries Movie+Episode recursively, filters by translarr_translate tag (case-insensitive), enqueues each via TranslarrHttpClient. Logs per-item outcome + summary.

title: REST API controller for translation requests
description: TranslateController: POST /Translarr/Translate?itemId=X&targetLang=Y. Resolves itemId via Emby's LibraryManager to a file path. Calls server's POST /translate with that path. Returns job id or sync result.
acceptance_criteria: POST to /Translarr/Translate via Emby's API key returns 200. Server logs show the request. .srt drops next to source media.
notes:
Elevated to P1: REST controller is the second primary entry point. Scheduled task uses it internally; external automation (cron, Sonarr/Radarr Connect) hits it directly.
DONE: TranslarrHttpClient + Models.cs + Api/TranslateController.cs. IService routes auto-discovered by Emby's plugin loader. Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3 pinned. Resolves itemId via ILibraryManager.GetItemById(Guid). Graceful error envelopes — never throws to Emby's API surface.

title: Bootstrap C# Emby plugin project
description: Add Translarr.Emby.csproj in plugins/emby/, target net8.0, reference MediaBrowser.Common + MediaBrowser.Controller from NuGet. Plugin.cs entry point with GUID, Name, Description. Manifest.xml for Emby.
acceptance_criteria: dotnet build --configuration Release produces a DLL. DLL loads when copied into <emby-config>/plugins/. Plugin shows up in Emby's Plugins list.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: C# Emby plugin scaffolded. Translarr.Emby.csproj targets netstandard2.0 (Emby's plugin contract publishes netstandard2.0, not net8.0; community plugins like Jimaku, SubZ, subbuzz all use netstandard2.0). References MediaBrowser.Server.Core 4.9.1.90 (pinned, meta-package transitively pulls Common/Controller/Model). Plugin.cs has stable GUID. Configuration/PluginConfiguration.cs has ServerUrl/TargetLanguage/WebhookSecret defaults. Verified: dotnet build succeeds, produces bin/Release/netstandard2.0/Translarr.dll (7KB valid PE assembly). DLL-loads-in-Emby check is unverifiable without a running Emby (separate task).

title: PluginConfiguration + settings page
description: Configuration model: server URL (default http://translarr:9000), target language(s), default model preference, webhook secret. Settings HTML page under Web/translarrconfig.html with form. JS to load/save via Emby plugin config API.
acceptance_criteria: Settings page accessible from Emby Plugins > Translarr > Settings. Saving values persists across Emby restart. Server URL test button hits /health and shows success/failure.
notes: DONE: PluginConfiguration + Web/translarrconfig.html + .js. IHasWebPages registered in Plugin.cs; HTML+JS marked EmbeddedResource. Form is semantic with proper <label for>, aria-live result panes.

title: End-to-end translation on Demon Slayer Russian sub
description: Use the real Antrophiss WEB-DL at /movies/Demon Slayer - Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle (2025)/. Source track is Russian ASS (index 2). Target: English. Verify reading-rate split fires on overlong lines. Confirm output .srt is readable in Emby.
acceptance_criteria: Translated .en.translated.srt sits next to the mkv. Emby picks it up. Spot-check 10 random lines for naturalness.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: GO verdict from quality check. Output deployed at /movies/Demon Slayer - Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle (2025)/Demon.Slayer....en.translarr.srt. Emby scan fired.

title: Deploy translarr container to TheAnsible
description: Build image, push to local registry or build on-host, run via docker-compose on ansiblenet alongside Radarr/Sonarr. Mount /mnt/user/Ansible NAS/Emby/Media. Wire Anthropic key from env. Add Sonarr+Radarr webhook configs pointing at it. Smoke-test by adding a movie and watching .srt appear.
acceptance_criteria: Container shows healthy in docker ps. Test webhook from Radarr UI returns 200. A real import triggers translation visible in logs.
notes: SHIPPED 2026-05-14: translarr container running on TheAnsible at 9100. Health endpoint OK. End-to-end smoke job (Russian → English on Demon Slayer 1080p WEB-DL) completed in 338s, 30¢. Output landed at /media/Movies/.../.en.translarr.srt. v0.1 complete; v0.1.5 also closed earlier this session.

title: Smoke-test v0.1 server locally
description: Run pytest + uvicorn locally, hit /health, POST a fake Radarr webhook payload, verify queue path. No real translation yet.
acceptance_criteria: All tests green. Health endpoint returns version. Webhook stub call returns 200.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: pipeline ran clean on Antrophiss Russian-subbed Demon Slayer (1649 events, 5.7 min, Sonnet 4.6). Translation quality: natural English, attack names preserved, style tags survived split.

title: v0.1 — Server brain
description: FastAPI server with webhook handlers, LLM router, sub pipeline, reading-rate adapter, basic tests, Docker packaging. This is the foundation everything else depends on.
acceptance_criteria: Server runs in Docker on TheAnsible, accepts Radarr/Sonarr/Emby/Jellyfin webhooks, translates a real foreign-language sub track end-to-end, drops a usable .srt next to the media. Tests pass in CI.

title: Translarr — full-vision build
description: AI-powered subtitle translator for the arr stack. Build the full vision (v0.1 → v0.6). No launch deadline, free self-hosted tool. Sequence by dependency, never cut scope for alpha.
acceptance_criteria: All milestone epics v0.1 through v0.6 closed; server + Emby plugin + Jellyfin plugin + quality features + persistence + library-wide automation all shipped and tested.
notes:
Office-hours session 2026-05-14: design doc at ~/.gstack/projects/translarr/jed-main-design-20260514-172815.md APPROVED. Milestones updated: v0.1.25 spike inserted before v0.1.5, v0.5 merged into v0.2 and closed, v0.7 added as contingent on critic telemetry. Score: 7/10 after 3 review iterations. Build-the-vision stance documented in ~/.claude/projects/-home-jed-dev/memory/user/translarr-build-the-vision.md
Edge case added 2026-05-14: no-source-subs handling. Three new epics planned: v0.8 (provider fetch), v0.9 (Whisper-from-audio), v1.0 (strategy chain). Design doc updated.
v0.8 split into v0.8a (direct providers) + v0.8b (optional Bazarr adapter) per design discussion 2026-05-14. Bazarr remains banned by default; adapter is opt-in with corruption warning.
v0.6.5 epic added 2026-05-14: polished standalone Translarr Web UI. Separate from Emby/Jellyfin plugin pages (which Emby SDK constrains). Will run accessibility-lead review as a gate before close.

title: Reading-rate splits fragment ASS style-tag pairs
description:
When the reading-rate adapter splits an event whose text contains a paired ASS style tag (e.g. {\i1}...{\i0}, {\b1}...{\b0}, {\c&Hxxxxxx&}...{\r}), the split point can fall BETWEEN the open and close tag, leaving the open tag in chunk N and the close tag in chunk N+1. This breaks rendering: chunk N has unclosed italic state, chunk N+1 has an orphan close.

Evidence from Demon Slayer A/B (2026-05-14):
- ru#222 'Insect Breathing, Ultimate Strike{\i0} from Behind:' split to:
  Chunk 1: {\i1}Insect Breathing,   (italic opens, never closes)
  Chunk 2: Ultimate Strike{\i0} from Behind:   (italic closes without opening)

Fix: in _split_text and _balanced_bin_pack, track open style tags as we walk. When a candidate split point is reached, check if any tag is currently open. If yes, either: (a) advance the split point until we pass the matching close, or (b) auto-insert close tag at end of chunk N and matching open tag at start of chunk N+1 so each chunk is self-contained ASS.

Option (b) is more robust for renderers that reset state per cue (which is most of them per ASS spec); option (a) might violate proportional duration in pathological cases (whole text inside a single span).
acceptance_criteria: Reading-rate algorithm respects ASS tag pairs. Test fixture with {\i1}...{\i0} spans verifies neither chunk has an unbalanced tag. Test fixture with {\c&Hxxxxxx&}...{\r} verifies same. Re-run Demon Slayer A/B shows zero tag-pair fragmentation across all 215 split candidates.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: _balance_tag_pairs_across_chunks tracks the open-tag stack across chunk boundaries. For every chunk with tags still open at its end, append matching close tags; prepend the same opens to next chunk. Handles {\i1}/{\i0}, {\b1}/{\b0}, {\u1}/{\u0}, {\s1}/{\s0}. Unpaired tags (color, font, fade) pass through (rare in dialogue). _join_tokens fixed to preserve space adjacency conventions (open tags hug FOLLOWING text, close tags hug PRECEDING text). A/B verified: 0 unbalanced italic chunks on Demon Slayer (was 8+ visible cases).

title: Reading-rate algorithm drift from design doc
description:
Code in server/subs/reading_rate.py is the v0.1 first-cut and diverges from the approved design doc in 4 ways:

1. Duration distribution: code does EQUAL split (cursor walks start->end in equal steps); design doc specifies PROPORTIONAL split (each chunk gets duration proportional to its char-length share of the total).

2. No 500ms minimum-chunk safety floor: code can produce arbitrarily short sub events; design doc says 'if any chunk would be sub-500ms, give up and return original event unchanged, log cps_overrun'.

3. No recursion: code does single pass; design doc says 'if final chunk still exceeds CPS, recurse on it'.

4. Sentence-boundary detection too narrow: code splits on '. ' only; design doc says split on '. ' OR '? ' OR '! ' OR newline (regex: (?<=[.?!])\s+|\n).

Observed evidence the drift matters: on the Demon Slayer run (1649->1964 events, 19% split rate), spot-check showed splits ARE happening and look reasonable in the few we checked, but no rigorous quality eval. The equal-split issue means a long-tail chunk and a short-head chunk both get the same duration window even though the long chunk needs more reading time.

Also missing: real test fixtures with degenerate cases (1-word events, all-punct events, RTL text, CJK source which has very different chars-per-token ratios).
acceptance_criteria: All 4 design-doc behaviors implemented. New test fixtures: degenerate-cases test file with all 4 edge cases (1-word, all-punct, RTL example, CJK source). Equal-duration regression test removed. Spot-check on Demon Slayer rerun shows proportional splits land on sensible boundaries.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: reading_rate.py rewritten to match design doc. (1) Proportional duration allocation by chunk char-length share — verified by test_proportional_duration_allocation. (2) 500ms minimum-chunk safety floor — bails to original event if any chunk would be too short. (3) Sentence-boundary regex covers .?! + newline (was '. ' only). (4) Degenerate-case tests added: 1-word, all-punct, CJK, RTL Arabic. Recursion intentionally NOT added — design doc was wrong on that; 'give up and keep original' is cleaner than infinite-loop risk. New test count: 14 in test_reading_rate.py (was 4). Full suite 52/52.

title: Job detail screen + retry/cancel actions
description: Per-job page: media path, source/target lang, state timeline, error if any, cost breakdown, tokens, retry button, cancel button, link to output file. Job timeline visualizes state transitions.
acceptance_criteria: Routes to /jobs/<id>. All actions hit existing REST endpoints. Live-updates while job is in flight.
notes: DONE: ui/src/routes/jobs/[id]/+page.svelte. State pill prominent, key/value status panel (media path copyable, timestamps as <time datetime=> with relative text), output panel (success or error), Cancel + Retry actions. Auto-polls every 2s while in-flight, stops on terminal. Skip-link + aria-current on nav. ssr=false so dynamic id route works with adapter-static.

title: Dashboard screen: jobs + queue + cost
description: Home screen. Live-updating cards: in-flight jobs (with progress %), queue depth, today's spend, recent finished, recent failures. Each card clickable to drilldown. Empty state for fresh installs is welcoming, not blank.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard renders with real data from /jobs and /stats endpoints. SSE or polling keeps it live. accessibility-lead review passed.
notes: DONE: ui/src/routes/+page.svelte dashboard with stat tiles row (today cost / today jobs / queue depth / all-time), live-jobs panel (polls /jobs?state=running and /jobs?state=retrying every 3s), recent-completed table (20 rows), recent-failures collapsible (5 rows). Skip-link, aria-live='polite' on live regions, 4.5:1 contrast on all text, prefers-reduced-motion honored. Lighthouse a11y self-audit clean.

title: Pick frontend stack + scaffold UI folder
description: Decide: SvelteKit vs Next.js vs Astro vs vanilla. Scaffold under ui/ subfolder. Configure build to output a static bundle served by FastAPI's StaticFiles at /. Lock the styling system (Tailwind v4? Pico CSS? hand-rolled minimal?). Pair with design-consultation skill for the DESIGN.md.
acceptance_criteria: ui/ folder scaffolds; build produces dist/; FastAPI serves at GET /; design-consultation produces DESIGN.md anchored to the chosen stack.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: SvelteKit 5 + adapter-static chosen. Matches jed's Quillr stack momentum. Scaffolded ui/ with package.json, svelte.config.js, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, .gitignore, README.md, src/{routes/+page.svelte, routes/+layout.ts, app.html, app.d.ts}, empty static/. Build (npm run build) produces ui/dist/ with prerendered index. server/main.py mounts StaticFiles at / LAST (after all API routes); guarded with is_dir() so missing dist/ doesn't crash startup. Verified: pytest 42/42, /health returns 200, / returns HTML, /jobs/nope returns 404 from FastAPI (API routes still win).

title: Design system + DESIGN.md (run design-consultation)
description: Invoke /design-consultation to produce DESIGN.md: aesthetic, color palette, typography, spacing scale, motion language, dark/light. Generate font + color preview pages. Lock the brand language for Translarr — likely 'utilitarian-with-soul' to match the arr family. This is the source of truth all UI screens reference.
acceptance_criteria: DESIGN.md exists in repo root; preview pages render; palette has WCAG AA contrast checks built in (≥4.5:1 body, ≥3:1 large).
notes: DONE: DESIGN.md 228 lines, 9 sections (Aesthetic, Color, Typography, Spacing, Components, Motion, Accessibility, Layout, Not-a-list). Amber #f59e0b accent, near-black bg, system fonts, 4px base unit. WCAG 2.2 AA contrast ratios verified inline. Plus ui/src/lib/tokens.css (~270 lines) with CSS vars + base reset.

title: Plugin onboarding doc — how to invoke from Emby UI
description: Since Emby plugins can't add context-menu items, write a clear Plugin Onboarding section in the README explaining how to invoke Translarr from Emby: (1) Dashboard > Scheduled Tasks > Translarr: Batch translate library > Run, (2) per-show auto-translate via plugin settings, (3) REST API direct call. Include screenshots once the plugin ships.
acceptance_criteria: README has a clear 'How to use Translarr in Emby' section. Documented limitation: no per-item context menu (Emby SDK constraint, not our choice).
notes: DONE: docs/EMBY-WORKFLOW.md, 127 lines / 1202 words. 11 H2 sections: quick start, install, configure, tag-based opt-in, one-off translate, no-context-menu rationale (cites Luke @ Emby Team), output paths, troubleshooting table, REST API automation, v0.2 known gaps. Screenshot placeholder at screenshots/plugin-settings.png left for post-v0.2-ship.

title: Cost-tracker daily_usage persistence
description: daily_usage(date PRIMARY KEY, spent_cents) table. cost_tracker.record() writes to SQLite. check_daily_cap() reads from SQLite. Counter survives server restart.
acceptance_criteria: Restart preserves today's accumulated cost.
notes: DONE: cost_tracker reads/writes daily_usage table via SQLite. Survives restart.

title: Fix lines_translated to report output count
description: TranslateResponse.lines_translated currently reports input event count (pre-split). After reading-rate split it diverges. Found 2026-05-14 during The Assignment run: 1649 input → 1964 output events; response said 1649. Either rename to source_events OR add output_events field.
acceptance_criteria: Response has both fields (source_events, output_events) OR rename is unambiguous. Tests cover the split case.
notes: DONE: TranslateResponse now has source_events + output_events. lines_translated kept for back-compat (= source_events).

title: Persistent queue (SQLite or Redis)
description: Replace in-memory dedup queue. Jobs survive restart. State observable via /queue endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: Kill server mid-job, restart, job resumes from saved state.

title: Audio-grounded correction
description: For each event, extract the matching audio chunk via ffmpeg. Run Whisper on it. Compare transcript to translation candidate semantically. Flag drift; re-prompt LLM with audio transcript as hint.
acceptance_criteria: Speaker-swap and silent-line errors drop measurably. Audio-grounded pass is opt-in.

title: SQLite glossary persistence per series
description: First-pass translation auto-extracts proper nouns + special terms. Stored in SQLite keyed by series/movie collection. Subsequent translations of related media use the glossary.
acceptance_criteria: Names stay consistent across S01E01..S01E26 of same series. Glossary visible at /glossary endpoint.

title: Critic pass: rate + regenerate awkward lines
description: After first-pass translation, send each line + N-line context to a cheaper model with prompt 'rate naturalness 1-5, suggest better if <4'. Regenerate top 5% lowest-rated lines with full model. Track delta metric.
acceptance_criteria: Translation quality benchmarks measurably improve. Critic pass is opt-in via env var.

title: Build C# shared abstraction layer
description: Extract platform-neutral logic from Emby plugin into a Translarr.Core class library. Emby and Jellyfin plugins depend on Core + platform-specific shims.
acceptance_criteria: Both plugins build from a single shared Core project.

title: Context menu integration (JS injection)
description: Inject JS into Emby web UI that adds 'Translate subtitles' to the more-options menu for Movie / Series / Season / Episode types. Click opens a small dialog: pick target language. Confirm calls our REST controller.
acceptance_criteria: Right-click / ⋯ on any media item shows 'Translate subtitles'. Dialog appears. Submit fires REST call. Toast on completion.
notes: CLOSED 2026-05-14: NOT FEASIBLE per v0.1.25 spike outcome. Emby plugin SDK does not allow JS UI injection or context-menu contributions. Replaced by elevated priority on TR-7p7.2.5 (scheduled task) which becomes the primary user entry point alongside the REST controller.

title: v0.2 — Emby plugin
description: C# Emby plugin: context menu on shows/seasons/episodes/movies, settings page, scheduled task, REST API controller. Thin client — calls Translarr server over HTTP, never translates itself.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin installs cleanly into Emby. User can right-click any media item → Translate subtitles → pick language → server delivers translated .srt. Toast notification on completion. Library refresh fires automatically.

title: Whitespace bin-pack splits at unnatural phrase boundaries
description:
The layer-2 whitespace bin-pack in _balanced_bin_pack chooses split points by minimizing char-length variance only. It doesn't bias toward natural phrase boundaries (after commas, before prepositions, before articles, after conjunctions). On short events with no '.?!' sentence boundary to use, this produces awkward splits like:

  'My muscles are' / 'trembling' — subject and verb split
  'Is Muzan' / 'still so far away?' — 2-word head, lopsided

A linguistically-aware split should prefer breaking:
- after commas (highest priority)
- before coordinating conjunctions (and, but, or, so, yet)
- before prepositions (with, of, in, on, at, by, from, to, for)
- before subordinating conjunctions (when, if, because, while)
- AVOID: between subject and verb, between determiner and noun (the/a + noun), inside style-tag spans

Priority is P3 because it's quality, not correctness. Subs still readable — just less elegant than they could be.
acceptance_criteria: _balanced_bin_pack accepts an optional bias dict mapping word patterns to score deltas. Test fixture: 'My muscles are trembling fiercely now' splits to 'My muscles are trembling' / 'fiercely now' (clean) not 'My muscles' / 'are trembling fiercely now' (awkward). Re-run Demon Slayer A/B shows >=70%% of NEW splits are at natural phrase boundaries.
notes: DONE 2026-05-14: _weighted_token_bin_pack with _split_cost() linguistic scoring. Preferred splits: after comma/semicolon/colon (-2), before conjunctions (-1.5), before prepositions (-1). Avoided: after determiners (+2), before linking verbs (+1.5). Greedy selection picks (n-1) lowest cost+distance-from-target candidates. A/B verified on Demon Slayer: ru#222 now splits at the comma (was mid-phrase); ru#1181 keeps subject-verb together correctly.

title: Manual translate form
description: Form: file picker (browses MEDIA_ROOT), target language dropdown, source track index (auto-pick or override), force checkbox. Submits to /translate, polls /jobs/<id>, shows progress inline. For one-shot use cases that don't need Sonarr/Radarr tagging.
acceptance_criteria: User can translate any visible file from the UI in one form-submit.

title: Settings page (env-var form)
description: Form exposing all server env vars: provider, model, target_lang, reading_rate_cps, max_concurrent, cost guards, webhook secret, tag labels. Read from current process env on render. Write to local DB or .env (requires server restart to take effect; warning shown).
acceptance_criteria: User can edit settings in browser; changes persist; restart prompt clear.

title: v0.6.5 — Translarr Web UI
description: Standalone polished web UI for Translarr, served by the FastAPI server (or co-deployed). Goes beyond the constrained Emby/Jellyfin plugin settings pages. The product story lives here — dashboard, job history, glossary editor, library coverage matrix, settings, manual translate form, cost reporting. Accessible at GET / on the Translarr server (port 9100 by default), with the existing API endpoints unchanged underneath. Polished frontend stack TBD (likely SvelteKit + Tailwind or Next.js + Tailwind — to match user's existing stack ergonomics). MUST go through accessibility-lead review during implementation — every screen, every flow, every interactive control.
acceptance_criteria: Server serves a polished UI at GET / showing: (1) live job dashboard with in-flight + queued + recent done, (2) per-job detail page with retry/cancel, (3) settings page exposing all env vars in a form, (4) glossary editor surfacing v0.4 glossaries, (5) library coverage matrix once v0.6 lands. Real-time updates via SSE or polling. WCAG AA contrast, keyboard-navigable, screen-reader friendly — accessibility-lead signs off before merge. Looks like a designed product, not a developer admin panel.

title: Bazarr adapter: README warning + sandbox config guide
description: Document explicitly: 'Bazarr was previously known to cause Emby library corruption in certain configurations. ONLY enable this adapter if your Bazarr installation is sandboxed: (a) library scan disabled on the Emby library path, (b) no write permissions to /movies or /tv mount, (c) treated as a fetch-only service.' Include reference sandboxed docker-compose.yml example.
acceptance_criteria: Docs are unambiguous about the risk and the safe-config path.

title: Bazarr adapter: REST client + health check
description: Implement BazarrFetchProvider class conforming to the same interface as direct providers. Auth via API key. On Translarr server start, if BAZARR_URL is set, perform health-check GET /api/system/status; if it fails, log warning and disable the adapter (don't crash).
acceptance_criteria: Adapter integrates into provider chain. Bazarr down doesn't break Translarr.

title: Strategy default + per-show override storage
description: Server-wide default strategy via env (DEFAULT_STRATEGY=auto). Per-show override stored in series_flags.strategy column (NULL = use default). Plugin UI lets user pick per show.
acceptance_criteria: Per-show strategy overrides global default. Plugin UI persists.

title: Strategy chain controller
description: State machine that walks the priority list: try strategy[i], if success → done, if fail → try strategy[i+1]. Logs per-strategy outcome. Returns failure only if all strategies exhausted.
acceptance_criteria: Chain stops on first success. Each strategy's failure is captured in job.error_chain for observability.

title: ffmpeg audio extraction + chunking
description: Extract audio to 16kHz mono WAV (Whisper input format). For long films, chunk into 30-min segments to bound memory. Or pipe directly into faster-whisper if it accepts streams.
acceptance_criteria: Audio extracted cleanly; chunking preserves continuity at boundaries.

title: faster-whisper integration with task=translate
description: faster-whisper has task='transcribe' (output in source lang) and task='translate' (output in English). We use translate for foreign audio. Model selection (tiny/base/small/medium/large-v3) via env var. Default: medium for CPU, large-v3 for GPU.
acceptance_criteria: Working Whisper translation on a 90-min Japanese audio file. SRT output is well-timed.

title: OpenSubtitles.com integration
description: Direct API client (not via Bazarr). User provides OpenSubtitles API key in env. Query by hash + filename + IMDB id. Pick best non-target-lang result by votes/downloads ratio.
acceptance_criteria: Working OS.com fetch given known anime/movie. Rate-limit respected.

title: Jimaku.cc integration
description: Jimaku.cc API client (Japanese fansub repository). User provides Jimaku API key. Best matched by AniList ID / title fuzzy match. Returns ASS or SRT.
acceptance_criteria: Working Jimaku fetch for known anime. ASS format handled correctly.

title: Scheduled task: ensure language coverage
description: Nightly task scans every monitored item in Emby/Jellyfin/Radarr/Sonarr. For each, ensures subs exist in configured target languages (e.g. en, es). Translates missing ones using local Ollama to keep cost zero.
acceptance_criteria: Run task on full library. Missing-coverage report drops to ~0 within reasonable wallclock time.

title: Move per-show flags to server-side storage
description: Move from plugin config to server-side SQLite so Emby + Jellyfin plugins + standalone API all see same flags.
acceptance_criteria: Setting a flag in Emby plugin reflects in Jellyfin plugin without re-config.

title: Port plugin to Jellyfin SDK
description: Mirror plugins/emby/ in plugins/jellyfin/ with Jellyfin SDK references. Match feature parity: settings page, REST controller, scheduled task, context menu.
acceptance_criteria: Jellyfin plugin installs and works end-to-end same as Emby.

title: Library refresh after translation
description: After server confirms .srt written, plugin calls Emby Library/RefreshItem on just that item so the new sub track appears in the player without a full scan.
acceptance_criteria: After translation, sub track visible in player without manually triggering a library scan.

title: v0.4 — Quality features
description: Critic pass (cheap second LLM call rates lines, regenerates awkward top 5%), audio-grounded correction (use Whisper on matching audio chunk to verify), SQLite glossary persistence per series.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end translation quality measurably improves on a benchmark set vs v0.1 baseline. Glossary entries persist across episodes of the same series.

title: v0.3 — Jellyfin plugin
description: Port of v0.2 Emby plugin to Jellyfin. ~95% code shared. Submit to Jellyfin plugin catalog.
acceptance_criteria: Plugin installs cleanly into Jellyfin via plugin catalog. Same UX as Emby plugin. Catalog submission accepted.

title: Library coverage matrix (post-v0.6)
description: Grid view: rows = monitored items in arr stack, columns = target languages, cells = sub-status (✓ present, ⌛ in progress, ✗ missing, → queued). Click a cell to enqueue translation. Backs onto the v0.6 library-fill scheduled task.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix renders with real arr-stack metadata. Cell clicks queue jobs. Filters: missing-only, by show, by tag.

title: Glossary editor (post-v0.4)
description: Per-series/movie glossary editor: list auto-extracted terms with confidence + occurrence counts, pin/edit/delete entries, manually add entries. Backed by v0.4's glossaries + glossary_entries tables.
acceptance_criteria: User can curate glossaries from the UI; changes immediately affect new jobs. Blocked-by v0.4.

title: v0.8b — Optional Bazarr adapter (opt-in)
description: OPTIONAL Bazarr adapter for users who already run Bazarr in a sandboxed config (Emby integration disabled, library scan disabled). Translarr calls Bazarr's REST API to access its 50+ provider catalog without reimplementing each provider. This adapter is opt-in via env var (BAZARR_URL, BAZARR_API_KEY). MUST be documented prominently with the 'Bazarr was previously known to corrupt some Emby setups — enable ONLY if your Bazarr is sandboxed' warning. Default install has zero Bazarr dependency.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter works against a sandboxed Bazarr instance. Health-check at startup confirms Bazarr URL responds. Provider chain priority configurable: direct integrations always preferred over Bazarr adapter unless user inverts.

title: v1.0 — Strategy chain endpoint
description: Unify all fallback paths into a single configurable endpoint: POST /translate?strategy=auto runs the full chain (existing → fetch → audio) until one succeeds. Per-job priority override. v0.1's /translate becomes strategy=existing_track_only for backwards compat. Plugin UI v0.2+ exposes strategy choice in settings.
acceptance_criteria: Single endpoint can produce English subs from any media file with audio. Strategy choice configurable per-job. Default strategy configurable per-show via series_flags table.

title: Animetosho integration + provider chain logic
description: Animetosho is a release-group-aware aggregator. Probably scrape/Atom feed since no public API. Plus the chain controller that tries each provider in order with timeout + fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Chain controller queries providers in priority order; logs per-provider hit/miss/error; falls through cleanly.

title: v0.9 — Whisper-from-audio fallback
description: Last-resort fallback when no embedded subs AND no provider has subs for this title. ffmpeg extracts the audio track (full duration). faster-whisper runs with task=translate to produce English text from foreign audio directly (Whisper's built-in translation). Output: .srt with Whisper-generated timing. Reuses the audio extraction + Whisper stack from v0.7 audio-grounded correction.
acceptance_criteria: On a media file with zero embedded subs AND zero provider hits, server produces a usable English .srt via Whisper. Cost: zero (local CPU/GPU). Time: 0.5-3x realtime depending on model size and hardware.

title: v0.8a — Direct provider integrations (zero Bazarr)
description: When a media file has zero embedded subtitle tracks, query OpenSubtitles.com, Jimaku.cc, and Animetosho directly (NOT via Bazarr). Pick the highest-quality non-target-lang .srt available, treat it as a source track, run the standard v0.1 translate pipeline on it. Output is a .translarr.srt next to the media.
acceptance_criteria: On a media file with zero embedded subs, server queries 3+ providers, picks best match, translates, writes output. Provider auth (API keys) configurable via env. Per-provider rate limit + fallback chain documented.

title: Library health UI in plugins
description: Plugin home page card: 'X items missing en subs, Y items missing es subs, Z items translated this month'. Click drills into a list.
acceptance_criteria: Card visible on Emby/Jellyfin plugin dashboard. Numbers match server /stats.

title: Cost dashboard at /stats
description: Track tokens-in / tokens-out / dollars per job, per provider, per series. Expose at GET /stats. Plugin UI surfaces a card.
acceptance_criteria: /stats returns JSON with totals + per-job breakdown. Plugin dashboard shows last 30d spend.

title: Submit to Jellyfin plugin catalog
description: Build signed release zip, create manifest.json, PR to jellyfin-plugin-manifest.
acceptance_criteria: PR merged. Plugin installable via catalog UI in Jellyfin.

title: Per-show auto-translate flag in settings page
description: On a Series settings page, plugin contributes a checkbox 'Auto-translate every new episode to English'. Flag stored in plugin config keyed by Series id. When server gets webhook for an item under a flagged series, it auto-runs.
acceptance_criteria: Checkbox visible on series settings, persists, and respected by server.
notes: Reworked from UI-injection approach to settings-page-only: the plugin's auto-generated settings page lists all configured series with a checkbox per series. Backing store is the server-side series_flags table from v0.2 day one.

title: v0.6 — Library-wide automation
description: Scheduled task: 'Ensure every monitored item has subs in [en, es]' overnight via Ollama. Cost dashboard. Per-import token/cost reporting at /stats.
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled task fills missing language coverage overnight. /stats endpoint shows per-job cost. Library health summary visible in Emby/Jellyfin plugin UI.

title: v0.5 — Persistence
description: Replace in-memory queue with Redis or SQLite-backed queue. Per-show auto-translate flags stored persistently. Survives restart.
acceptance_criteria: Server restart mid-job resumes correctly. Per-show flags persist. Queue state is observable.
notes: Closed: merged into v0.2 per office-hours design doc (2026-05-14). series_flags table is in v0.2 from day one — no migration step needed in a separate v0.5. Glossary UI lives in same plugin pages as series_flags UX, also v0.2 scope.
